tournaments.title: Neon Rift Arena: Night Qualifier
tournaments.description: A precision duel tournament set inside controlled rift arenas above the city skyline. Players win through timing, positioning, and clean mechanical reads rather than chaos. The qualifier uses short best-of-three sets, live bracket reseeding, and a final played under low-visibility arena rules.

tournaments.title: Chrome Drift Circuit: Midnight Grid
tournaments.description: A high-speed hover racing bracket where every corner is scored by line control, speed retention, and clean overtakes. Racers compete through time trials before the top crews advance into head-to-head elimination runs inside the midnight tunnel grid.

tournaments.title: Signal Ghost Protocol: Black Node Cup
tournaments.description: A tactical stealth competition focused on route planning, silent captures, and information denial. Squads move through simulated server complexes where every sound, scan, and decoy matters. The cleanest extraction times decide the final seed.

tournaments.title: Voidline Syndicate: Control Room Open
tournaments.description: A cerebral strategy event built around territory pressure, resource timing, and long-form adaptation. Players draft syndicate tools before each match, then fight for control of a living city map that shifts between rounds.

tournaments.title: Synthblade Zero: Rooftop Masters
tournaments.description: A minimalist one-on-one duel circuit where spacing, parries, and tempo define every round. Competitors advance through a Swiss stage into a single-elimination final played on mirrored rooftop arenas with strict stamina rules.

tournaments.title: Datafall Eclipse: Silent Drop
tournaments.description: A survival arena tournament with shrinking signal zones, limited sensor windows, and quiet endgame rotations. Teams are rewarded for smart positioning, clean retreats, and decisive final engagements instead of pure aggression.

tournaments.title: Mirror City Heist: Vault Series
tournaments.description: A stylish team heist bracket built around synchronized routes, silent alarms, and split-second vault control. Teams alternate attacker and security roles, with bonus points for clean exits and low-exposure objective play.

tournaments.title: Arcology Nine: Pre-Match Invitational
tournaments.description: A squad arena invitational inside a vertical city arcology. The format favors clean team rotations, objective discipline, and calm late-round execution. Eight invited squads meet one open qualifier winner in the final bracket.
